items.correct: Designing products usable by the widest possible range of people without special adaptation.
items.random_wrong: Adding multiple language options to packaging.
items.why: Students conflate inclusive with specialist design. Inclusive design widens MAINSTREAM products to work for more people - the OXO Good Grips kitchen tools, designed for arthritic hands, work better for everyone.

items.q: What does 'Rethink' mean in the 6 Rs?
items.correct: Questioning whether a product is necessary or could be designed differently for sustainability.
items.misconception: Trying a design again if it does not work first time.
items.why: Students apply 'rethink' to design iteration. In sustainability, it means challenging the NEED for a product altogether, or fundamentally rethinking how it's made - a more radical step than reducing materials.

items.correct: A drawing showing multiple 2D views (front, side, plan) of an object.
items.misconception: A 3D drawing showing the object at an angle.
items.random_wrong: A drawing in black ink only.
items.why: Students confuse orthographic (multi-view 2D) with isometric (single 3D view). Orthographic projects each face flat onto separate views - used for engineering specification.
The answer examiners want is: Computer Aided Design. “Crafted Art Design” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's 3D printing. “Word docs” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many CAD softwares are named in this lesson?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
